topics.title: Keyboard shortcut to reset the game at any time
topics.trigger: Players had no quick way to restart the snake game mid-session without navigating away or waiting for a game-over screen.
topics.response:
- Added a Shift+R keyboard shortcut in `src/main.ts` that triggers a full game reset from any state. The handler checks for an uppercase `R` keypress (which the browser reports when Shift is held) and calls the existing new-game routine immediately.
- Fixed a visual bug in `src/main.ts` where the pause overlay remained visible after resetting. A single line now explicitly hides the pause indicator whenever a new game starts.
- Updated the on-screen control hint in `index.html` to include "Press Shift+R to Reset" so players can discover the shortcut without consulting documentation.
topics.decisions:
- **Shift+R over plain R**: Using Shift+R rather than an unmodified key avoids accidentally resetting the game when a player is typing or when the R key might be repurposed for another action. The trade-off is a slightly higher input cost, but the safety benefit outweighs the minor friction for a destructive action like discarding a live game.
- **Reusing the existing new-game routine**: Rather than writing a dedicated reset path, the shortcut calls the same startup function already used by the game-over screen. This keeps reset behavior consistent across all entry points and avoids duplicating state-cleanup logic, at the cost of no opportunity to add reset-specific behavior (such as a confirmation prompt) in the future.

recap: Players can now reset the snake game at any point during a session by pressing Shift+R, without waiting for a game-over screen. The control hint displayed below the game canvas was updated to advertise the shortcut, making it discoverable on first play. A pre-existing visual glitch where the pause overlay stayed on screen after starting a new game was also resolved as part of this work.
